_instructions: Add your work experience here. Copy the experience object for each job.

experiences.title: AI/ML Engineer
experiences.description: Building production RAG and automation on internal reporting data — owning architecture, evaluation, and the workflows that put analytics in non-technical hands.
responsibilities: Built a production RAG assistant using OpenAI APIs, LangChain, and a Pinecone vector store, enabling non-technical stakeholders to query analytics in natural language and reducing ad-hoc analyst requests by 35%.

Designed chunking strategy, embedding selection, and prompt templates (system prompts, few-shot, guardrails) with structured evaluation (faithfulness, answer relevance, retrieval precision), lifting response accuracy from 62% to 88% on a curated ground-truth set.
Owned data architecture for 3 production databases across 5+ concurrent projects — schema design, indexing, and integrity constraints that cut downstream engineering corrections by 15%.
Designed 10+ Python and SQL automation workflows replacing manual reporting, recovering 20 analyst-hours per month and improving data freshness to near real time.
Profiled production logs to isolate bottlenecks and shipped targeted fixes for a 10% performance gain and 25% fewer critical errors, with full regression coverage.

experiences.title: AI/ML Engineering Intern
experiences.description: Built ML services and pipelines from scratch — anomaly detection, GCP model pipelines, and ETL redesign across a subscriber base of millions.
responsibilities: Built a Python anomaly detection service from scratch using Isolation Forest with threshold-based fallbacks, improving hardware fault detection accuracy by 25% and network reliability by 20% across millions of subscribers.
Developed and evaluated ML pipelines on GCP (BigQuery, Vertex AI), driving team-wide migration off legacy analytics tooling through enablement sessions across 3+ teams.
Redesigned 8 ETL pipelines in Palantir Foundry as automated, idempotent workflows — 30% faster processing and new data-reliability standards adopted org-wide.
Designed A/B testing infrastructure for ML-driven feature evaluations, bringing statistical rigor to rollout decisions previously based on intuition.

experiences.title: Programmer Analyst
experiences.description: Architected data-quality and forecasting systems at scale on AWS — profiling, validation, and demand models across millions of records.
responsibilities: Architected an end-to-end data quality system with AWS Glue and Python across 12 interdependent datasets — automated profiling, schema validation, and anomaly flagging that improved pipeline efficiency by 41% and cut downtime by 15%.

Designed forecasting models in Python and Amazon Athena over 500,000+ records, improving demand-planning accuracy by 10% and contributing to an 8% quarterly revenue uplift by surfacing signals the incumbent model ignored.
Standardized cross-functional data definitions and delivery cadence across 4 teams, cutting reporting turnaround by 25%.
